text: Hey, everyone. This is Mario Leyva. Just a couple questions for you here. So you've gone to this step on your parasophageal hiatal hernia. Are you closing the hiatus with permanent suture or using pledges or using absorbable PDS, Vicro? What are you using? Let me know what you're using on this and what your reasoning is.
Also are using a bougie what bougie size are using and if you don't use a bougie what is your mark to confirm that you're not too tight or too loose on your repair.
Do you utilize any techniques to identify any sutures or anatomy if you had to come back for a redo down the line? And if so, what are they?
Okay, you've gone to this step. Are you going to buttress the repair with anything? Are you going to use a biologic? Are you going to use a permanent synthetic mesh? What are you going to use here?

Well, as you see here, I used a Gore IOA product for the support for the hiatal hernia. However, I recently found that there's several other types of grafts that you can use here. I'm curious to see which ones you've used and why you like it and so forth. I've heard some really good things about the Biodesign Heidelhernia graft from Cook Biotech. Also some Phasics and I know some people have done some synthetic uses of mesh here. In addition, other people have not done anything, so I'm curious to hear and give me your reasoning. In addition, once you've deployed whatever graft you're going to use, how are you suturing in place? Here I'm just using a Vicryl. Reasoning is I'm thinking it's going to dissolve away in six months or so, so there's no need for a permanent suture. However, I'm curious to see what your thoughts are.

After the repair has been completed and you placed your graft in, how exactly are you going to do the nissen fundoplication? Are you doing a full fundoplication? Are you doing a partial? And how exactly are you suturing it in place and keeping it together?

After you've completed the fundoplication, how exactly are you assessing your RAP or are you assessing the RAP? Are you doing an EGD or are you just doing it visually? Do you do anything specific on this? In addition, do you do a swallow test the next day or are you just going off clinically and are you starting to feed immediately? And what is your diet regimen? Please answer these questions. Let's get some discussion going on this.
